response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is a poor fit for this C++-centric industrial-automation role. His C++ exposure is minimal and over a decade old — limited to a six-month contract at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, and a bachelor-thesis audio project. The role demands deep, current C++ and STL expertise as its core technical requirement. He has no industrial automation domain knowledge, no experience with IEC 62443 or SDL security practices, and no familiarity with the industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) that are central to the position.
strengths: Extensive distributed and event-driven architecture experience, including a micro-services platform handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic
Demonstrated agile discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — plus active mentoring and workshop facilitation showing team-leadership capability
Broad polyglot background with REST API design, IoT-adjacent geo-data work, and cloud exposure (AWS CDK), indicating architectural adaptability
concerns: C++ proficiency is marginal and at least 10 years out of date; the entire active career is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, not C++ or STL
Zero industrial automation, OT systems, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle implementation or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards, which are non-negotiable requirements for this role

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or OT protocol knowledge
Distributed systems and event-driven architecture experience
